text:
There once was a little girl named Sophie who loved collecting stones. Every day after school, she would explore the forest just beyond her village, collecting stones that caught her eye. Some were smooth and shiny, like polished obsidian. Others were rough with holes like melons. Her favorite was a tiny pebble that she thought might one day fit inside a shell.

One summer day, while wandering alone, Sophie found something she hadn’t yet discovered—Ironically, it wasn’t a stone. It was a peculiar-looking creature. Its body was covered in plates, each one a different metallic hue. It had two small wings, one near each shoulder, which fluttered softly as it went about its business.

Sophie watched as the creature settled beside a small spring. With a grace out of place for its spiny body, it dipped its plates into the water and drank.

Curious, Sophie cautiously approached. The creature tilted its head toward her. Startled, she spoke to it. “
